fields.description:
ABOUT CLAY

Our mission is to help organizations turn any growth idea into reality.

We see growth as a creative practice, not a formula. Finding and reaching your best-fit customers takes unique ideas and constant iteration. As AI makes execution faster and tactics easier to copy, creativity is the only lasting advantage. We're already helping thousands of customers https://clayrun.notion.site/Wall-of-Love-b243f2b67607438b9fad99341e6b8d47 — including Anthropic, Notion, Google, and Ramp — go to market with unique data, signals, and AI research.

In 2025, we raised a $100M Series C https://www.nytimes.com/2025/08/05/business/dealbook/clay-ai-marketing-fundraise.htmlbacked by world-class investors including Sequoia, CapitalG, and First Round — and crossed $100M in revenue.

In 2026, we announced our second employee tender offer https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/28/business/dealbook/clay-start-up-tender-offers.html in 9 months at a new $5B valuation. We also launched a community equity round https://www.clay.com/blog/community-equity-offering, for our customers, agency partners, and club members.

Some things to know about us:

 - Our community http://community.clay.com includes 11,000+ customers, 150+ integration partners, 125+ agencies, 50+ Clay clubs https://luma.com/claylive, and 30k members on Slack.

 - Our culture https://nextplayso.substack.com/p/spotlight-clay is unique inside and outside of work. Our team members are also DJs, activists, writers, clowns, marathoners, skydivers, psychedelic therapists, social workers, and more.

 - All employees can work for free with world-class coaches who specialize in creativity, management, and more.

 - Our operating principles — including negative maintenance and non-attached action — guide our work. Read more about them here https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/61477f2c24a826836f969afe/685d83a71452245cc1129791_4d770abfd83e276ec15315a2e06945bd_Clay2025_OperatingPrinciples.pdf.

 - Read about us in the NYT https://www.nytimes.com/2025/08/05/business/dealbook/clay-ai-marketing-fundraise.html, Forbes http://google.com/search?q=forbes+clay&rlz=1C5OZZY_enUS1155US1155&oq=forbes+clay&gs_lcrp=EgZjaHJvbWUyBggAEEUYOTIHCAEQABiABDIHCAIQABiABDIHCAMQABiABDIHCAQQABiABDIHCAUQABiABDIHCAYQABiABDIHCAcQABiABDIHCAgQABiABDIHCAkQABiABNIBBzkzM2owajSoAgOwAgHxBVAe8UAxJx_p&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8, First Round Review https://review.firstround.com/podcast/inside-clays-unconventional-path-to-1-25b/, and more https://www.clay.com/press.

Hear from our employees directly on our Glassdoor https://www.glassdoor.com/Overview/Working-at-Clay-EI_IE9850794.11,15.htm page!


DATA PARTNERSHIPS @ CLAY

We’re looking for a Technical Partnerships Engineer to join our Data and Integration Partnerships team. In this role, you’ll be the technical backbone of our partner ecosystem—owning the end-to-end lifecycle of data provider and platform integrations, from scoping and building to launching and optimizing.

A core part of this role is data quality and evaluation: building the frameworks and processes to rigorously test, benchmark, and monitor our data providers. You’ll help us answer critical questions like “Which provider has the best email coverage for SMB?” and “Is this new enrichment source worth adding?”

You’ll work directly with partners like Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, and dozens of data enrichment providers to bring new capabilities to Clay’s customers. This is a high-impact, cross-functional role where you’ll collaborate with Product, Engineering, Go-to-Market, and external partner teams.





WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Integration Development & Maintenance
   
   - Write clean, well-documented code (primarily TypeScript/Node.js) for API integrations
   
   - Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues, working closely with partner technical teams
   
   - Monitor integration health, performance, and data quality

 - Data Testing & Evaluation
   
   - Build and maintain testing frameworks to evaluate data provider quality, accuracy, and coverage
   
   - Design benchmarking methodologies to compare providers across key dimensions (fill rates, accuracy, freshness, cost-efficiency)
   
   - Run structured evaluations when onboarding new data providers or renegotiating existing partnerships
   
   - Create dashboards and reporting to track provider performance over time
   
   - Partner with Product and Engineering to establish data quality standards and SLAs
   
   - Identify gaps in our data coverage and recommend providers or solutions to fill them

 - Partner Technical Enablement
   
   - Serve as the primary technical point of contact for strategic partners
   
   - Lead technical discovery calls and scoping sessions with prospective partners
   
   - Create technical documentation, implementation guides, and best practices for partners
   
   - Support partner developers building on Clay’s platform

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration
   
   - Partner with Product and Engineering to inform roadmap priorities based on partner and customer feedback
   
   - Work with GTM teams to enable sales motions around new integrations
   
   - Contribute to internal tooling that improves partner onboarding and integration management

 - Ecosystem Growth
   
   - Evaluate new data providers and integration opportunities
   
   - Prototype and validate new integration concepts quickly
   
   - Stay current on the data enrichment and sales tech landscape


WHAT YOU'LL BRING:

 - 3+ years of experience in a technical role (software engineering, solutions engineering, technical partnerships, or similar)

 - Strong programming skills in TypeScript/JavaScript; experience with Node.js

 - Deep familiarity with REST APIs, OAuth, webhooks, and integration patterns

 - Experience working with third-party APIs and navigating partner technical documentation

 - Comfort with data analysis—able to write SQL, build dashboards, and draw insights from messy datasets

 - Excellent written and verbal communication—you can translate complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences

 - Self-starter mentality; comfortable with ambiguity and context-switching


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience building data quality frameworks, testing pipelines, or evaluation methodologies

 - Background in data enrichment, GTM tools, or the broader RevTech ecosystem

 - Experience at a high-growth B2B SaaS company

 - Familiarity with CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot) and sales/marketing tech stacks

 - Experience with Clay as a user or builder

 - Prior partnerships or business development experience
fields.title: Technical Partnerships Engineer
